name: Profile_R5_ActorDefinition_R4
title: Cross-version Profile for R5.ActorDefinition resource for use in FHIR R4
description: This cross-version profile allows R5 ActorDefinition resource content to be represented via FHIR R4 Basic resources.

element.short: Cross-version Profile for R5.ActorDefinition resource for use in FHIR R4
element.definition: This cross-version profile allows R5 ActorDefinition resource content to be represented via FHIR R4 Basic resources.

element.short: R5: Canonical identifier for this actor definition, represented as a URI (globally unique) (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.url` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.url` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Can be a urn:uuid: or a urn:oid: but real http: addresses are preferred.  Multiple instances may share the same URL if they have a distinct version.

The determination of when to create a new version of a resource (same url, new version) vs. defining a new artifact is up to the author.  Considerations for making this decision are found in [Technical and Business Versions](https://hl7.org/fhir/resource..html#versions). 

In some cases, the resource can no longer be found at the stated url, but the url itself cannot change. Implementations can use the [meta.source](https://hl7.org/fhir/resou.html#meta) element to indicate where the current master source of the resource can be found.

element.short: R5: Business version of the actor definition (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.version` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.version` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
There may be different actor definition instances that have the same identifier but different versions.  The version can be appended to the url in a reference to allow a reference to a particular business version of the actor definition with the format [url]|[version]. The version SHOULD NOT contain a '#' - see [Business Version](https://hl7.org/fhir/resource.h.html#bv-format).

element.short: R5: How to compare versions (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.versionAlgorithm[x]` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.versionAlgorithm[x]` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
If set as a string, this is a FHIRPath expression that has two additional context variables passed in - %version1 and %version2 and will return a negative number if version1 is newer, a positive number if version2 and a 0 if the version ordering can't be successfully be determined.

element.short: R5: Name for this actor definition (computer friendly) (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.name` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.name` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
The name is not expected to be globally unique. The name should be a simple alphanumeric type name to ensure that it is machine-processing friendly.

element.short: R5: Name for this actor definition (human friendly) (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.title` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.title` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
This name does not need to be machine-processing friendly and may contain punctuation, white-space, etc.

element.short: R5: For testing purposes, not real usage (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.experimental` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.experimental` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Allows filtering of actor definitions that are appropriate for use versus not.

element.short: R5: Date last changed (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.date` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.date` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
The date is often not tracked until the resource is published, but may be present on draft content. Note that this is not the same as the resource last-modified-date, since the resource may be a secondary representation of the actor definition. Additional specific dates may be added as extensions or be found by consulting Provenances associated with past versions of the resource.

See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](https://hl7.org/fhir/canonicalreso.html#localization).

element.short: R5: Name of the publisher/steward (organization or individual) (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.publisher` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.publisher` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Usually an organization but may be an individual. The publisher (or steward) of the actor definition is the organization or individual primarily responsible for the maintenance and upkeep of the actor definition. This is not necessarily the same individual or organization that developed and initially authored the content. The publisher is the primary point of contact for questions or issues with the actor definition. This item SHOULD be populated unless the information is available from context.

element.short: R5: Contact details for the publisher (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.contact` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.contact` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
May be a web site, an email address, a telephone number, etc.

See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](https://hl7.org/fhir/canonicalreso.html#localization).

element.short: R5: Natural language description of the actor (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.description` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.description` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
This description can be used for a general description of the actor, and which covers why it was defined.

element.short: R5: The context that the content is intended to support (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.useContext` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.useContext` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
When multiple useContexts are specified, there is no expectation that all or any of the contexts apply.

element.short: R5: Intended jurisdiction for actor definition (if applicable) (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.jurisdiction` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.jurisdiction` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Element `ActorDefinition.jurisdiction` has been flagged as deprecated.
It may be possible for the actor definition to be used in jurisdictions other than those for which it was originally designed or intended.

DEPRECATION NOTE: For consistency, implementations are encouraged to migrate to using the new 'jurisdiction' code in the useContext element.  (I.e. useContext.code indicating http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/usage-context-type#jurisdiction and useContext.valueCodeableConcept indicating the jurisdiction.)

element.short: R5: Why this actor definition is defined (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.purpose` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.purpose` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
This element does not describe the usage of the actor definition. Instead, it provides traceability of ''why'' the resource is either needed or ''why'' it is defined as it is.  This may be used to point to source materials or specifications that drove the structure of this actor definition.

element.short: R5: Use and/or publishing restrictions (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.copyright` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.copyright` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
...

element.short: R5: Copyright holder and year(s) (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.copyrightLabel` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.copyrightLabel` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
The (c) symbol should NOT be included in this string. It will be added by software when rendering the notation. Full details about licensing, restrictions, warrantees, etc. goes in the more general 'copyright' element.

element.short: R5: person | system (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.type` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.type` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Functionality associated with the actor (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.documentation` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.documentation` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
In general terms, the description desribes what the actor is, and this element describes what the actor does (or is expected to do).

element.short: R5: Reference to more information about the actor (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.reference` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.reference` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: CapabilityStatement for the actor (if applicable) (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.capabilities` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.capabilities` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Definition of this actor in another context / IG (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.derivedFrom` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.derivedFrom` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: draft | active | retired | unknown (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.status` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.status` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Allows filtering of actor definitions that are appropriate for use versus not.

See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](https://hl7.org/fhir/canonicalreso.html#localization).
